reason: The Rainmaker shows an underdog lawyer taking on a cold-hearted insurance giant.
A Time to Kill pits a small-town lawyer against systemic bigotry and corporate power.
Disclosure finds a lone executive fighting sexual and corporate corruption in the boardroom.
Clear and Present Danger follows an idealistic DOJ lawyer up against hidden government forces.
Eraser features an honest attorney battling a corrupt corporation to uncover the truth.
An American Werewolf in London has a lawyer detective chasing corporate-backed terror.
Maverick’s gamble in the courtroom against a big bank feels tense and scrappy.
Priest sees one man fighting an oppressive regime without any legal muscle on his side.
Clear and Present Danger embodies the small-lawyer-versus-big-power conflict perfectly.
Disclosure remains a classic for its quiet fight against overwhelming corporate might.
